'''Q-3PO''' was a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] that worked as a [[Species/Legends|species]] relations [[Droid/Legends|droid]] for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Later he was given to [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Deena Kinnet]], after she became a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biography==
During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Q-3PO was a species relations droid for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. However, due to the droid's many years in foreign and hostile environments, his [[Memory bank/Legends|memory core]] became riddled with [[virus]]es. Seemingly destined to the scrap yard, an unidentified Jedi—with the help of [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—took on the task and successfully repaired him.<ref name="CWA" />

Q-3PO was coated with white enamel that gave it a more "medical" appearance. It was actually a collection and transmission droid from which Deena got her information.

Early in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Q-3PO was stationed on an Imperial [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. Here, he helped an unknown [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] take a shuttle back to his home.

==Behind the scenes==
Before the New-Game Enhancements of [[2005]], Q-3PO actually appeared as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|Non-Player Character]] in the [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''. He was part of a tutorial which helped players get accustomed to the game. He would bring players to a planet of their choice to end the tutorial. He was later removed, however, when the [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]] became the new in-game tutorial. Outside the game, Q-3PO was the forum handle for Star Wars Galaxies' first community relations manager, Kevin O'Hara, who left the team after the [[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed|''Jump To Lightspeed'']] expansion released in 2004.

Q-3PO made a minor appearance in [[Sony]] Online Entertainment's 2010 Star Wars [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online game|MMO]], [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (video game)|''Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures'' video game]] in the minigame Droid Programming.<ref name="CWA" />
